metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (with proving command `pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_session_log_capture.py -q`) that explicitly verifies the session_log consumer tests are green after the REBAR-first precedence-inversion update.
findings.finding: The plan requires updating `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_session_log_capture.py` to reflect the REBAR-first precedence inversion (called out in both the 'What' and 'Scope' sections), but no AC and no proving command verifies those tests are green after the change. An executor could skip or break this update and pass all stated ACs.
findings.location: Scope section (audit+update of two enumerated test consumers) and 'What' bullet: 'Audit the enumerated consumer tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_session_log_capture.py'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC7-equivalent: 'The session_log consumer tests in `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_session_log_capture.py` pass under the new REBAR-first order. Proof: `pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_session_log_capture.py -q` exits 0 (all updated cases green).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the `test_session_log_capture.py` audit obligation is accurate: since every fingerprinting test there explicitly clears `REBAR_SESSION_ID` before asserting on `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, no expectation conflicts with the new REBAR-first order — clarify whether any test actually needs updating or remove the false 'update for OLD precedence' directive.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that tests in `test_session_log_capture.py` 'set/clears both `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` and `REBAR_SESSION_ID`' and that 'any case whose expectation depends on the OLD (CLAUDE_CODE-first) precedence' needs updating — but reading the actual test file shows `REBAR_SESSION_ID` is only ever cleared (delenv'd), never set to a positive value, and no test sets both vars simultaneously. Every fingerprinting test explicitly clears `REBAR_SESSION_ID` before using `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, so the behavior is identical under both the old (CLAUDE_CODE-first) and new (REBAR-first) orderings. No test expectation there actually conflicts with the new order, making the 'audit+update' directive for this file rest on an incorrect codebase assertion.
findings.location: Scope section — 'Audit+update of the two enumerated test consumers'; What section — 'Audit the enumerated consumer tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_session_log_capture.py'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the audit instruction to: 'Read `test_session_log_capture.py`; confirm no test sets both `REBAR_SESSION_ID` and `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` simultaneously — if none do, no expectation updates are required (all tests clear REBAR_SESSION_ID before asserting on CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, which behaves identically under both orderings). Only add a new test if coverage of the simultaneous-both-set case is desired.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the `test_session_log_capture.py` audit outcome: confirm that no existing test in that file sets both `REBAR_SESSION_ID` and `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` simultaneously, so the 'update any case whose expectation depends on OLD precedence' instruction is satisfied by a no-op (zero edits needed), and document this explicitly in the plan or as an implementation note.
findings.finding: The plan prescribes auditing `test_session_log_capture.py` for 'any case whose expectation depends on the OLD (CLAUDE_CODE-first) session_log precedence', but inspection of that file shows that every test which sets CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID first calls `monkeypatch.delenv("REBAR_SESSION_ID", raising=False)` — so no test assertion actually depends on CLAUDE_CODE winning over REBAR_SESSION_ID. After the refactor the file's tests will continue to pass without modification. The plan's instruction to update that file for precedence-dependent expectations is technically vacuous as-written, creating confusion for the implementer about what updates are actually required.
findings.location: What section / 'Audit+update of the two enumerated test consumers' + Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to the Scope/What section: '(audit confirms: no test in test_session_log_capture.py simultaneously sets both vars, so zero precedence-dependent assertions exist — no edits required there beyond the existing delenv coverage)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the `_SESSION_ID_VARS` list abstraction (vs. a plain `or`-chain) is justified by S5 being a committed next story, or simplify to a plain `or`-chain in S1 and convert to a list when S5 lands.
findings.finding: The `_SESSION_ID_VARS = ["REBAR_SESSION_ID", "CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID", "SESSION_ID"]` data-driven list design (iterating a list rather than a plain `or`-chain) is motivated in the plan primarily by the deferred S5 story (`privy-eloquent-nauplius`), which adds `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID`/`CODEX_THREAD_ID`. At S1 ship time there are exactly 2 call sites, so the list mechanism serves a hypothetical (S5 extensibility) rather than the current done-definition. A plain `or`-chain in `resolve_session_id()` would satisfy all S1 ACs equally and would be trivially swapped for a list when S5 lands.
findings.location: What section — `_SESSION_ID_VARS` definition; Scope — 'Deferred to S5 (c557)' note

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
